name: keeper
description: Local MCP server for the Keeper Security vault and MSP/enterprise admin console (via Keeper Commander).
long_description: Search and read vault records, list folders and shared folders, browse enterprise nodes/users/teams/roles, run compliance, BreachWatch, security-audit and event reports, manage users (invite, lock/unlock, team and role membership, vault transfer), approve devices, and execute any Commander command. Wraps the Keeper Commander CLI, so it uses Keeper's zero-knowledge model and a one-time persistent-login bootstrap. macOS: launched via uv against a local Python environment. Requires a Keeper Commander persistent-login config.

tools.name: keeper_msp_info
tools.description: Show MSP licenses and managed companies.

tools.name: keeper_enterprise_role_info
tools.description: Show one role's policies and members.

tools.name: keeper_add_user_to_team
tools.description: Add a user to a team.

tools.name: keeper_remove_user_from_team
tools.description: Remove a user from a team.

tools.name: keeper_add_user_to_role
tools.description: Add a user to a role.

tools.name: keeper_run_command
tools.description: Run any non-interactive Commander command (escape hatch).

keeper_config_file.title: Keeper Commander config file
keeper_config_file.description: Path to the Commander persistent-login config created by the one-time bootstrap (see README). Defaults to ~/.keeper/config.json.

keeper_command_timeout.title: Per-command timeout (seconds)
keeper_command_timeout.description: Commander logs in and syncs on each call; large vaults may need more. Defaults to 120.
